FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TreeReconciler

Namespace
FluentGpu.Reconciler
Assembly
FluentGpu.Reconciler.dll

Patches the retained SceneStore from an immutable Element tree. Signals-first: every component is a reactive render-effect (re-renders + reconciles ONLY its own subtree when its state/context changes — granular, never the whole app); fine-grained bindings (TransformBind/OpacityBind/…) and reactive control-flow (ShowEl/ ForEl) are effects too. The keyed positional+type diff is retained as the STRUCTURAL engine (used on re-render and behind For/Show); a reused component on a parent re-render is a no-op (it is autonomous).

public sealed class TreeReconciler

scene SceneStore
strings StringTable
runtime ReactiveRuntime

Properties

Anim

Set by the host; injected into each component so animation hooks can seed tracks on their node.

public AnimEngine? Anim { get; set; }

Property Value

AnimEngine

ComponentCount

Mounted component entries (the _comps anchor map) — O(1) census.

public int ComponentCount { get; }

Property Value

int

ImageEpoch

Set by the host; bumped on any image status change so UseImage consumers re-render granularly.

public IReadSignal<int>? ImageEpoch { get; set; }

Property Value

IReadSignal<int>

Images

Set by the host; image nodes request decodes through it and pin/unpin for residency (liveness).

public ImageCache? Images { get; set; }

Property Value

ImageCache

LiveComponents

Live nested components — the host drains their effects each frame.

public List<Component> LiveComponents { get; }

Property Value

List<Component>

NodeBindingCount

Nodes carrying reactive bindings / control-flow effects (the _nodeBindings map) — O(1) census.

public int NodeBindingCount { get; }

Property Value

int

ProviderCount

Context-provider value signals (the _providerSig map) — O(1) census.

public int ProviderCount { get; }

Property Value

int

Runtime

The reactive scheduler — one per host; signals schedule render-effects/bindings here, the host flushes it.

public ReactiveRuntime Runtime { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveRuntime

VirtualBoundaryCount

Virtual-list viewport boundaries (the _virtuals map) — O(1) census.

public int VirtualBoundaryCount { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

ConsumeReconciled()

True (and reset) if any mount/update/remove happened since the last call — the host's "layout needed" gate.

public bool ConsumeReconciled()

Returns

bool

ConsumeRenderCount()

Number of component render-effects that ran since the last call (proves granular re-render in tests).

public int ConsumeRenderCount()

Returns

int

MountRoot(Component)

Mount a root COMPONENT as a reactive render-effect (the host path): it renders into Scene.Root and re-renders itself (only) when its own state/context changes.

public void MountRoot(Component root)

Parameters

root Component

ReRealizeVirtuals()

Re-realize any virtual-list windows flagged VirtualRangeDirty (scroll boundary crossing) — granular, no component re-render. Called by the host each frame.

public bool ReRealizeVirtuals()

Returns

bool

ReconcileRoot(Element, Element?)

Imperative full reconcile of an explicit element tree (tests / non-host callers).

public void ReconcileRoot(Element newRoot, Element? oldRoot)

Parameters

newRoot Element
oldRoot Element

SetAmbient(object, Signal<object?>)

Publish an ambient context (e.g. Viewport.Size) as a host-owned signal consumers can read.

public void SetAmbient(object channel, Signal<object?> sig)

Parameters

channel object
sig Signal<object>
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
